title: botrychium subbifoliatum
intro: Botrychium subbifoliatum, also known as the two-leaf grapefern, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the grapefern family, native to the United States and Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Botrychium subbifoliatum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a potentially valuable natural remedy for skin and tissue injuries. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to create teas, infusions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential.
